markets.question: New Rihanna Album before GTA VI?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Rihanna officially releases a new album before Grand Theft Auto VI is officially released in the US. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If neither occurs by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to 50-50.

For the purposes of this market, "release" refers to the game becoming publicly available for purchase or download in the US. Early access, beta versions, other forms of pre-release availability, or leaks will not count as an official release. If the release is only for certain consoles (e.g. Xbox Series X/S) it will count.

The resolution source for the release of GTA VI is official information from Rockstar Games or its parent company, Take-Two Interactive.

Officially released means that a newly released album is officially available for download or streaming (not including live events) by the resolution date.

Re-releases, deluxe editions, compilations, or greatest hits albums will not count, unless they contain at least 50% tracks that have never been officially released in any format prior to the album's release.

The resolution source for the album release will be any official streaming or download site, e.g. Apple Music or Spotify.

events.title: What will happen before GTA VI?
events.description: This is a market on predicting events that will occur before the release of Grand Theft Auto VI.

eventMetadata.context_description: Rockstar Games and Take-Two have locked Grand Theft Auto VI for a November 19, 2026 release on PlayStation 5 and Xbox Series X/S following two prior delays, leaving roughly five months for traders to assess which events will land first. Industry momentum centers on Rockstar’s anticipated summer 2026 marketing push, including a potential third trailer and the final GTA Online DLC, which historically precede major launches. Broader gaming schedules are shifting around the title to avoid direct competition, while cultural milestones—such as music album drops, AI model releases like GPT-6, and geopolitical developments—factor into market-implied odds. Traders weigh confirmed timelines against the game’s history of polish-driven slips and the wisdom of crowds reflected in real-capital positions.

markets.question: Will Harvey Weinstein be sentenced to no prison time?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the prison sentence, if any, imposed on Harvey Weinstein as part of his ongoing New York retrial (People of the State of New York v. Harvey Weinstein) by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

This market will resolve immediately based on the first sentence rendered in this case, regardless of any appeals. If Weinstein is found not guilty, there is a mistrial, or if the first sentencing does not include any jail or prison time, this market will resolve to "No Prison Time."

If no sentencing takes place by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will also resolve to "No Prison Time."

If the sentence falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

For the purposes of this market, it does not matter whether the sentence is concurrent or consecutive to any existing sentence Weinstein is already serving. The market will resolve based on the total prison sentence imposed in this case.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the New York court system or other involved U.S. government sources; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: **Recent mistrials and procedural developments have driven trader consensus toward “No Prison Time” at 82%.** Harvey Weinstein’s 2020 New York conviction was overturned, and the 2025 retrial produced a mixed verdict with one conviction carrying up to 25 years, an acquittal, and a hung jury on the Jessica Mann rape count. The May 2026 retrial on that same count ended in another mistrial after jurors deadlocked, with reports indicating nine of twelve favored acquittal. Prosecutors must decide by late June whether to pursue a fourth trial. Weinstein, now 74 and in declining health, has already served more than five years; any additional New York sentence could run concurrently with his 16-year California term, raising the likelihood of little or no further incarceration. These repeated hung juries and the slim prospects for a decisive conviction have positioned “No Prison Time” as the dominant outcome while leaving modest probability on the 20–30-year range if prosecutors secure a conviction and consecutive time.

markets.question: Will the Carolina Hurricanes win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Carolina Hurricanes win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will resolve to “No” if it becomes impossible for this team to win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup based off the rules of the NHL.

The resolution source for this market will be information from the NHL.

events.title: 2026 NHL Stanley Cup Champion
events.description: This market is to predict the winner of the 2025–26 NHL Stanley Cup championship.

eventMetadata.context_description: Carolina Hurricanes hold an 79.5% implied probability as 2026 Stanley Cup favorites due to their commanding 3-2 series lead over the Vegas Golden Knights in the Final and a dominant postseason run that includes just one loss through the Eastern Conference Finals. The Canes posted the Eastern Conference's top record at 53-22-7 with 113 points, showcasing elite puck possession, power-play efficiency, and goaltending adjustments such as the mid-series switch to Brandon Bussi. Jordan Staal's multi-goal contributions and expanded role have further tilted momentum. Vegas remains alive at 19.7% implied odds after strong regular-season territorial play and a deep playoff path, but must win the next two games on home ice to force a decisive Game 7. Trader consensus reflects Carolina's series advantage and historical comeback barriers for trailing teams in best-of-seven formats.

markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the New York Knicks win the 2026 NBA Finals. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will resolve to “No” if it becomes impossible for this team to win the 2026 NBA Finals based off the rules of the NBA.

The resolution source for this market will be information from the NBA.

events.title: 2026 NBA Champion
events.description: This market is to predict the winner of the 2025–26 NBA Finals.

eventMetadata.context_description: The New York Knicks hold an 81.5% implied probability in the 2026 NBA Champion market due to their commanding 3-1 lead in the NBA Finals against the San Antonio Spurs. New York secured this advantage with a dramatic Game 4 comeback victory at Madison Square Garden, erasing a 29-point deficit to win 107-106 and move within one victory of the title. The Knicks finished the regular season at 53-29 as the East’s No. 3 seed before navigating a strong playoff path that included a conference finals sweep, while the Spurs (62-20) entered as Western Conference runners-up behind Victor Wembanyama but have now dropped three of four games in the series. This series score and recent momentum shift drive the current trader consensus reflected in the probabilities.

markets.question: Will MegaETH perform an airdrop by June 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if MegaETH launches a token and performs an airdrop by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purposes of this market "locked" tokens or non-swappable tokens will not suffice to resolve this market to "Yes". Airdrops of NFTs will not qualify.

The primary resolution source for this market is on-chain information and official information from MegaETH, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

markets.question: Will Spain win the 2026 FIFA World Cup?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the national team that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Spain and France sit atop the 2026 World Cup winner market as narrow co-favorites because both enter with elite squad depth, proven major-tournament experience, and strong recent international results. Spain’s Euro 2024 triumph and core of young talents such as Lamine Yamal anchor its position, while France’s generational attacking options and defensive organization sustain its implied probability near 16 percent. Portugal and England remain within striking distance thanks to star-laden rosters featuring Cristiano Ronaldo and Jude Bellingham respectively, plus favorable group-stage positioning. Brazil and defending champion Argentina trail slightly but retain realistic knockout paths through individual quality. The tight clustering among the top five reflects the absence of a single dominant side and the high variance inherent in a 48-team field where early results and injuries can rapidly shift trader consensus.

eventMetadata.context_description: Spain and France sit atop the 2026 World Cup winner market as narrow co-favorites because both enter with elite squad depth, proven major-tournament experience, and strong recent international results. Spain’s Euro 2024 triumph and core of young talents such as Lamine Yamal anchor its position, while France’s generational attacking options and defensive organization sustain its implied probability near 16 percent. Portugal and England remain within striking distance thanks to star-laden rosters featuring Cristiano Ronaldo and Jude Bellingham respectively, plus favorable group-stage positioning. Brazil and defending champion Argentina trail slightly but retain realistic knockout paths through individual quality. The tight clustering among the top five reflects the absence of a single dominant side and the high variance inherent in a 48-team field where early results and injuries can rapidly shift trader consensus.

eventMetadata.context_description: Spain and France sit atop the 2026 World Cup winner market as narrow co-favorites because both enter with elite squad depth, proven major-tournament experience, and strong recent international results. Spain’s Euro 2024 triumph and core of young talents such as Lamine Yamal anchor its position, while France’s generational attacking options and defensive organization sustain its implied probability near 16 percent. Portugal and England remain within striking distance thanks to star-laden rosters featuring Cristiano Ronaldo and Jude Bellingham respectively, plus favorable group-stage positioning. Brazil and defending champion Argentina trail slightly but retain realistic knockout paths through individual quality. The tight clustering among the top five reflects the absence of a single dominant side and the high variance inherent in a 48-team field where early results and injuries can rapidly shift trader consensus.

markets.question: Xi Jinping out before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if China's General Secretary of the Communist Party, Xi Jinping, is removed from power for any length of time between July 3, 2025, and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

CCP General Secretary Xi Jinping will be considered removed from power if he announces his resignation from his role as General Secretary, or is otherwise dismissed, detained, disqualified, or otherwise loses his position or is prevented from fulfilling his duties as General Secretary within this market's timeframe.

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

events.title: Xi Jinping out before 2027?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if China's General Secretary of the Communist Party, Xi Jinping, is removed from power for any length of time between July 3, 2025, and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

CCP General Secretary Xi Jinping will be considered removed from power if he announces his resignation from his role as General Secretary, or is otherwise dismissed, detained, disqualified, or otherwise loses his position or is prevented from fulfilling his duties as General Secretary within this market's timeframe.

eventMetadata.context_description: Xi Jinping maintains firm control over the Chinese Communist Party and military as of mid-2026, with analysts widely expecting him to secure a fourth term at the 21st Party Congress in 2027 rather than exit power beforehand. Recent military purges targeting senior figures, including Politburo member Zhang Youxia, have further centralized authority under Xi without evident internal opposition or factional challenges. Official actions such as his June 2026 state visit to North Korea and policy directives for the 2026-2030 period underscore ongoing leadership continuity. Speculation about health issues or early succession has not altered the trader consensus reflected in the 93% "No" probability, as structural factors like the lack of an anointed successor and Xi's dominance over elite institutions point to extended tenure through the near term.

markets.question: Will Gavin Newsom win the 2028 Democratic presidential nomination?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the named individual wins and accepts the 2028 nomination of the Democratic Party for U.S. president. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democratic Party sources.

Any replacement of the democratic nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Gavin Newsom leads the early 2028 Democratic presidential nomination field in trader consensus at 23 percent, ahead of Jon Ossoff and Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez, as the party rebuilds after 2024. His position reflects sustained national visibility as California governor, term-limited status freeing him for a full campaign, and recent moves emphasizing populist stances on regulation and affordability that test appeal beyond coastal bases. Ossoff and Ocasio-Cortez draw support from Senate and House records plus regional or ideological strength, while Kamala Harris and Josh Shapiro benefit from prior national exposure. A fragmented primary field with no formal declarations yet means support could consolidate around candidates demonstrating strength in early-state polling, donor networks, endorsements from party leaders, and turnout among key voting blocs during the 2027–2028 cycle.
